raw_output.summary: Five robustness findings identified across the codebase. The most impactful are: (1) toast DOM elements that leak permanently when CSS animations don't fire, (2) the entire application startup silently failing with no user feedback due to discarded Promise rejections in `main.js`, and (3) a potential re-entrant `disconnect()` loop in the Yjs provider close guard. Two further findings cover loss of diagnostic information when a Web Worker compile error occurs and when an image paste upload fails silently.

findings.title: Toast elements leak into DOM when CSS animation does not fire
findings.description: In `ToastController.show`, the toast element is only removed when the `animationend` event fires. If the CSS animation never plays â€” for example when `prefers-reduced-motion` is active, when stylesheets have not loaded, when the container is hidden, or when the element is detached before the animation completes â€” `animationend` will never be dispatched and the event listener registered with `{ once: true }` will never execute. This means toast `<div>` elements accumulate in the DOM for the lifetime of the page.
findings.recommendation: Add a fallback removal path that does not depend on the CSS animation. A simple fix is to schedule a second `setTimeout` with the expected animation duration plus a small buffer as a guaranteed teardown. For example, after adding the `animationend` listener also call `setTimeout(() => toast.remove(), animationDurationMs + 100)`. Using `toast.isConnected` as a guard prevents a double-remove error.

findings.title: Application startup errors silently discarded via `void start()`
findings.description: In `src/client/main.js`, both code paths call `void start()`, which intentionally discards the returned Promise. If `ensureClientAuthenticated()` rejects, or if the `CollabMdAppShell` constructor or `app.initialize()` throws, the error is silently lost â€” producing only an unhandled-promise-rejection warning in the browser console with no user-visible feedback. The application will appear to do nothing, with no error surface to diagnose the failure.

findings.title: Re-entrant `disconnect()` call inside `connection-close` handler may loop
findings.description: In `stopReconnectOnControlledClose`, `provider.disconnect()` is called synchronously from within the `connection-close` event handler. Depending on the Yjs WebSocket provider implementation, calling `disconnect()` may itself emit a new `connection-close` event â€” potentially preserving the same close reason from the original server frame. If that happens the handler fires again, calls `disconnect()` again, and the cycle repeats. There is no guard such as a `disconnecting` boolean or a check that the provider is already in the disconnected state.

findings.title: Worker compile errors are reported as strings, losing stack traces and causal chains
findings.description: In `src/client/application/preview-render-worker.js`, when `compilePreviewDocument` throws, only `error.message` (a plain string) is posted back to the main thread. The full `Error` object â€” including `stack`, `cause`, and any custom properties â€” cannot be transferred across `postMessage` boundaries because `Error` instances are not structured-cloneable in all environments. When a render failure occurs the main thread receives only a bare message string, making it significantly harder to diagnose the root cause, especially for errors that are re-thrown with chained causes.

findings.title: Image paste upload failures are silently swallowed with no user feedback
findings.description: In `handleImagePasteEvent`, when `onImagePaste(imageFile)` rejects, the rejection is caught with a `console.error` only. The user receives no toast or visible indication that the pasted image failed to upload. The paste event's default has already been suppressed via `event.preventDefault()` at that point, so the paste is consumed but the image silently disappears.
findings.recommendation: Expose an `onError` callback parameter alongside `onImagePaste` and invoke it when the promise rejects, enabling callers to surface a toast notification. Alternatively, allow the rejected reason to propagate up to the consumer that already holds a reference to the `ToastController`.
